decisions.title: Use SQLite
decisions.summary: The database uses SQLite for its operations.

decisions.title: CSS Guidelines
decisions.summary: Provides all decisions and conventions for CSS styling used in the Task Manager project

decisions.title: Use Flask for web application
decisions.summary: The web application is built using the Flask framework.

decisions.title: Use SQLite for database
decisions.summary: SQLite is chosen as the database management system.

decisions.title: Use Jinja2 for HTML templates
decisions.summary: Jinja2 is used for rendering HTML templates.

decisions.title: Implement factory patterns for object creation
decisions.summary: Use factory patterns for object creation

decisions.title: Use context managers for resource cleanup
decisions.summary: Implement resource cleanup using context managers

decisions.title: Apply dependency injection for testability
decisions.summary: Use dependency injection to improve testability

rules.notes: Test endpoints with various scenarios: valid input, missing required fields, invalid IDs, database errors, filter combinations, and concurrent requests.

When receiving task data via POST/PUT, validate required fields, check priority and status are in valid sets, use Task dataclass for model validation, and return 400 Bad Request with error details if invalid.
Use try-except blocks for error handling, return appropriate HTTP status codes, and don't expose internal error details to the client.

Cache statistics endpoint results, limit task list pagination, use database indexes for frequently filtered columns, avoid N+1 queries by prefetching related data, and compress JSON responses for large lists.
Set appropriate CORS headers, validate and sanitize all user input, use werkzeug security utilities for passwords, and log error details server-side.
Always use parameterized queries with ? placeholders to prevent SQL injection.
Do not use string formatting or f-strings for SQL queries.
Use the _row_to_task() helper to convert database rows to objects, centralizing mapping logic.

rules.rule: Use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS
rules.notes: Use CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for idempotent table initialization.
Define all constraints explicitly when creating tables.
Include meaningful column comments, especially when using migrations.

rules.rule: Use ALTER TABLE for Adding New Columns
rules.notes: Use ALTER TABLE in the create_schema() function when adding new columns to the tasks table.

rules.rule: Use IF NOT EXISTS for Optional Columns
rules.notes: Use the IF NOT EXISTS clause when adding optional columns to the tasks table.

rules.rule: Update Row Mapping for New Fields
rules.notes: Update the _row_to_task() function to map new fields when adding them to the tasks table.
Update the save_task() function's INSERT/UPDATE statement when adding new fields to the tasks table.
Document any changes made to the schema.

rules.rule: Create Indexes for Frequently Queried Columns
rules.notes: Create indexes on frequently queried columns (e.g., id, status, priority) to improve performance.
Use transactions for batch operations to ensure data integrity.
Commit transactions frequently but not excessively to balance performance and data integrity.
Use the VACUUM command periodically on large databases to optimize space usage and performance.

rules.rule: Use In-Memory SQLite for Unit Tests
rules.notes: Use in-memory SQLite (:memory:) for unit tests to isolate test environments.
Test the behavior of transaction rollbacks to ensure data integrity.
Test for constraint violations to ensure data integrity.
Verify data integrity after database operations to ensure consistency.
When modifying the Task class, adhere to the provided structure guidelines.
Always validate priority against the allowed values: 'low', 'medium', 'high', 'critical'.

rules.rule: Maintain consistency between status and completed fields
rules.notes: Keep the 'status' and 'completed' fields in sync for backward compatibility.
Check for duplicates, maintain order, and store tags as a JSON array in the database.
All public methods for converting Task to/from dict must use asdict() from the dataclasses module.
Keep selectors simple and avoid overly specific rules
Base font size 16px, headings 1.5–2x
Responsive breakpoints at 600px and 900px
Use a 8px scale (`.m-1` = 8px margin, `.p-2` = 16px padding, etc)

rules.rule: Use `.button` class with modifiers for buttons
rules.notes: Use `cursor: pointer;` and `transition: background-color 0.2s`
Full width, 8px padding, border-radius 4px, border `1px solid #ccc`
Stack columns and make buttons full-width

rules.rule: Use SVG icons inline or via `` with class `.icon`
rules.notes: Fixed size 24px

rules.rule: Structure CSS with variables for easy switching to dark mode
rules.notes: Not currently supported
The project follows a layered architecture with Presentation, Business Logic, Data Model, and Data Persistence layers.
The Task data structure is defined using dataclasses with type hints.
Input validation is performed at the Task dataclass, TaskManager, API endpoints, and database levels.
Errors are propagated with context, allowing for better debugging and handling.

rules.rule: Write unit tests for new methods
rules.notes: Write unit tests for new methods to ensure functionality
Test edge cases like empty inputs, None values, and invalid data
Ensure high test coverage to catch potential issues

rules.rule: Use pytest as the test framework
rules.notes: Utilize pytest for testing
Adhere to PEP 8 guidelines for consistent code style
Limit line length to improve readability
Avoid single-letter variable names except for loop counters
Include type hints for better code documentation and type safety
Prevent SQL injection by using parameterized queries

rules.rule: Wrap database calls in try-catch blocks
rules.notes: Handle database errors gracefully

rules.rule: Implement connection cleanup in finally blocks
rules.notes: Ensure proper resource cleanup
Provide detailed error logs for debugging

rules.rule: Use database indexes for frequently queried columns
rules.notes: Improve query performance with indexes
Optimize database operations by batching

rules.rule: Limit in-memory task lists to reasonable sizes
rules.notes: Manage memory usage for task lists
Improve performance with caching

rules.rule: Always validate input before persisting to database
rules.notes: Ensure data integrity by validating input

rules.rule: Use explicit try-catch blocks with meaningful error messages
rules.notes: Handle errors explicitly with clear messages

rules.rule: Use type hints on all functions and class methods
rules.notes: Enhance type safety with type hints

rules.rule: Keep functions focused on single responsibilities
rules.notes: Promote modularity and maintainability

rules.rule: Add docstrings to all public methods
rules.notes: Improve code documentation with docstrings
Use PascalCase for class names
Use snake_case for function and method names
Use UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for constant names
Prefix private member names with an underscore
Aim for 80% coverage
